article.headline: Virginia Tech 60, Georgetown 55
article.story: WASHINGTON (AP) -- Bryant Matthews scored 26 points, including nine of Virginia Tech's final 12, in the Hokies' 60-55 victory over Georgetown on Saturday. Brandon Bowman scored 19 points for the free-falling Hoyas (13-14, 4-12 Big East), who gained a spot in the Big East tournament later Saturday when West Virginia beat Miami. Zabian Dowdell added 14 points for Virginia Tech. Georgetown had a 55-53 lead with 2:40 to play. However, a traveling call on Bowman allowed the Hokies (14-13, 7-9) to find Matthews open on the wing for the 3-pointer that put Virginia Tech ahead for good. Matthews scored on an offensive rebound to put Virginia Tech up by three with 1:25 to play and the Hokies hit two more free throws inside the final 33 seconds to seal the win. The Hoyas led by as many as seven in the first half and by as many as five in the second, but failed to score just five points over the final 6:21 of the game.
